meta.note: PDF files verified to be exports of the two markdown sources. phase: 1=foundational (days 1-7), 2=applied (days 8-14), 3=expert/integration (days 15-21).

concepts.name: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
concepts.summary: Systems performing tasks that normally need human intelligence; modern AI is neural networks trained on massive datasets — sophisticated pattern matching.

concepts.name: Neural Network
concepts.summary: Layered mathematical structure of weights adjusted during training so the network predicts outputs from inputs (Input → Layer 1 → ... → Output).

concepts.name: Training vs Inference
concepts.summary: Training adjusts billions of weights on data+answers (expensive, occasional); inference runs the trained model to answer (cheap, every query — what Ollama does locally).

concepts.name: Parameters / Weights
concepts.summary: The numbers inside a neural network; a '7B model' has 7 billion. More parameters = more patterns learnable but more RAM/VRAM and slower.

concepts.name: LLM (Large Language Model)
concepts.summary: Transformer trained to predict the next token; from that one task on internet-scale data it learns grammar, facts, reasoning, code. Generates one token at a time using the context window.

concepts.name: Transformer Architecture
concepts.summary: The 2017 Google architecture behind all modern LLMs; key innovation is self-attention — every token can attend to every other token in context.

concepts.name: Self-Attention
concepts.summary: Mechanism scoring each word against every other word so relevant words link up (resolves 'it' = the cat, not the mat).

concepts.name: Context Window
concepts.summary: Max text an LLM holds in working memory at once (qwen2.5:14b ≈128K tokens; BGE-large 512 tokens/chunk). Beyond it, earlier content is forgotten.

concepts.name: Tokens
concepts.summary: Basic LLM unit, roughly a word-piece. ~1 token ≈ 0.75 words; 1 page ≈ 750 tokens.

concepts.name: Tokenization (BPE / WordPiece / SentencePiece)
concepts.summary: Converting text to tokens before the model sees it; different models use different tokenizers (BGE-large uses WordPiece, max 512 tokens — longer text truncated).

concepts.name: Temperature
concepts.summary: Randomness control: 0.0 deterministic, 0.7 balanced, 1.0+ creative. Agents use ~0.1 so structured THOUGHT/ACTION output stays reliable.

concepts.name: Quantization (FP16 / Q4 / GGUF)
concepts.summary: Compressing a model by lowering weight precision: FP16 ≈2 bytes/param, Q4 ≈0.5 (4x smaller); 70B at 2-bit fits ~22GB RAM. Smaller/faster but slightly less accurate.

concepts.name: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
concepts.summary: Post-training where humans rate outputs and the model learns to produce higher-rated responses — makes LLMs helpful and safe (ChatGPT, Claude, instruction-tuned models).

concepts.name: Ollama
concepts.summary: Local LLM runtime exposing an HTTP API at localhost:11434; runs GGUF quantized models, auto-uses GPU, no internet required.

concepts.name: Embedding
concepts.summary: Converting text/images to a vector of numbers representing meaning; similar meanings land close together ('dog' and 'puppy' vectors nearly identical).

concepts.name: Embedding Model (BGE-large-en-v1.5)
concepts.summary: Network trained only to produce good embeddings (not generate text). BGE-large: 1024-dim, contrastive learning on text pairs, uses a query prefix for retrieval.

concepts.name: Cosine Similarity
concepts.summary: Angle-based vector similarity: 1.0 identical meaning, 0 unrelated, -1 opposite. cos(θ) = (A·B)/(|A|×|B|). Used for retrieval, cache hits (0.92), MMR.

concepts.name: Vector Space
concepts.summary: The N-dimensional space embeddings live in (BGE 1024-dim, CLIP 512-dim); similar content clusters, so search = nearest neighbours.

concepts.name: CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining)
concepts.summary: OpenAI 2021 model embedding text AND images into the same vector space via image-caption pairs — enables text→image and image→image search (clip-ViT-B-32, 512-dim).

concepts.name: Sentence Transformers library
concepts.summary: Python library (UKP Lab) wrapping embedding models like BGE and CLIP: model.encode([text]) → numpy array.

concepts.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
concepts.summary: Give LLMs external knowledge: chunk → embed → store; at query time embed the query, fetch similar chunks, add to prompt, generate. Lewis et al. (Meta), 2020.

concepts.name: RAG vs Fine-tuning
concepts.summary: Fine-tuning bakes knowledge into weights (expensive, static); RAG retrieves fresh content at query time (cheap, dynamic, always current) — better for knowledge.

concepts.name: Chunking (size + overlap)
concepts.summary: Splitting documents before embedding (512 words, 128-word overlap). Overlap keeps sentences spanning boundaries intact; needed for token limits and precision.

concepts.name: Section-Aware Chunking
concepts.summary: Split by document structure (headings) before word count so chunks carry their section name ('3. AVAILABILITY'), not just a page number.

concepts.name: Chunk Metadata
concepts.summary: Data stored beside each chunk: source, title, section, page, content_type (text/image/table) — enables filtering and citations.

concepts.name: Vector Database
concepts.summary: Database optimised for storing/searching high-dimensional vectors with specialised indexes — SQL can't do nearest-neighbour in 1024-dim efficiently.

concepts.name: LanceDB
concepts.summary: Rust vector DB with Python bindings: columnar storage, dual vectors per row, hybrid search, persistent .lance files, serverless (~8ms/query vs ~45ms ChromaDB).

concepts.name: ChromaDB
concepts.summary: SQLite-based vector DB the project replaced with LanceDB — simpler but slower, no native hybrid search, no dual vectors.

concepts.name: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)
concepts.summary: Multi-layer graph index for fast approximate vector search: start sparse at top, zoom into denser layers (continent→country→city). O(log N), ~99%+ accurate.

concepts.name: Approximate Nearest Neighbour (ANN)
concepts.summary: Finding 'close enough' vectors without checking every one; HNSW is one algorithm — others: IVF, FAISS, ScaNN. Trades exactness for speed.

concepts.name: Dual-Vector Schema
concepts.summary: Each row holds text_vector (1024-dim BGE, meaning) and image_vector (512-dim CLIP, visual) — same chunk findable by text query OR visual similarity; image fields nullable.

concepts.name: Persistence / Durable Storage
concepts.summary: LanceDB writes to disk immediately (lance_db/<category>/rag.lance); data survives restarts and reboots — no in-memory-only mode.

concepts.name: Semantic Search
concepts.summary: Finding content by meaning, not exact words — 'how does scaling work?' matches 'horizontal vs vertical scale-out'. Powered by embeddings + cosine similarity.

concepts.name: Keyword / Full-Text Search (FTS)
concepts.summary: Traditional exact-word/stem search ranked by BM25. Catches exact terms semantic search misses; misses synonyms.

concepts.name: Hybrid Search
concepts.summary: Vector (semantic) + keyword (FTS) fused in one query — semantic finds meaning, keyword finds literal values like '99.99%'.

concepts.name: Multi-Query Retrieval
concepts.summary: Run 3 phrasings (original, key-terms-only, domain-boosted) and merge — catches content expressed differently from how the user asked.

concepts.name: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)
concepts.summary: Merge multiple ranked lists fairly: score = Σ 1/(rank+60) across lists; the 60 stops top items dominating. Consistent mid-rankers beat one-list wonders.

concepts.name: MMR (Maximum Marginal Relevance)
concepts.summary: Diversity-aware selection: score = λ×relevance − (1−λ)×max similarity to already-picked (λ=0.5) — five different angles instead of five copies.

concepts.name: Table-Aware Routing
concepts.summary: Queries mentioning table/chart/list/comparison boost content_type='table' chunks to the front so structured questions get structured data.

concepts.name: Factual Anchor Injection
concepts.summary: Post-retrieval fix for specific facts: detect patterns (99.XX%), score pool candidates for co-occurrence of the value + a time figure, inject the best match into results.

concepts.name: Two-Stage Retrieval (bi-encoder → cross-encoder)
concepts.summary: Stage 1: bi-encoder embeds query and chunk separately — fast, less nuanced. Stage 2: cross-encoder reads the pair together — slow but accurate, on a small candidate set.

concepts.name: Cross-Encoder
concepts.summary: Model scoring a (query, document) pair read together through attention — much more accurate than vector compare but O(N) per query. ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2, trained on MS MARCO.

concepts.name: BM25
concepts.summary: 'Best Match 25' keyword ranking: term frequency × inverse document frequency with document-length normalisation. Improved TF-IDF; basis of most FTS.

concepts.name: Multimodal AI
concepts.summary: AI processing multiple input types (text+images+audio). LLaVA = language+vision; CLIP = image+text in one space.

concepts.name: LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant)
concepts.summary: Open multimodal model: CLIP vision encoder → projection layer → Llama/Vicuna LLM. Takes image+prompt, outputs description; used for charts/diagrams; ~2-5s per image via Ollama.

concepts.name: Vision Encoder
concepts.summary: Network converting an image to a fixed vector (LLaVA uses CLIP's ViT, 768-dim) projected into the LLM's token space so the LLM can 'see'.

concepts.name: ViT (Vision Transformer)
concepts.summary: Transformer on images: split into patches, each patch a 'token', then self-attention. ViT-B/32 = base size, 32×32-pixel patches.

concepts.name: OCR vs Vision AI
concepts.summary: OCR extracts text via pattern matching (fast, clean print only); Vision AI (LLaVA) understands meaning — describes charts, explains diagrams — slower but far more capable.

concepts.name: Base64 Encoding
concepts.summary: Binary→ASCII conversion so images travel over JSON/HTTP (needed for Ollama API); ~33% size overhead, so resize to 1024px first.

concepts.name: AI Agent (agentic properties)
sections: Guide §11 What Makes an AI Agentic
concepts.summary: LLM that takes actions in a loop until done: autonomous, goal-directed, tool-using, multi-step, self-correcting, observable — beyond single-turn Q&A.

concepts.name: ReAct Pattern
concepts.summary: Reason+Act alternation (Yao et al. 2022): THOUGHT → ACTION → INPUT → OBSERVATION, repeat, then FINAL_ANSWER. Like a detective reasoning between clues.

concepts.name: Tool / Skill
concepts.summary: A callable function defined by name, description (the LLM reads it to decide usage), parameters, implementation. The project has 15 skills (web_search, rag_query, calculator, ...).

concepts.name: Prompt Engineering
concepts.summary: Designing instruction text: role, current date/time, skill list with examples, exact format rules, behavioural rules. Bad prompt = unpredictable agent.

concepts.name: Structured Output
concepts.summary: Making the LLM emit machine-parseable output; the project extracts ACTION/INPUT/THOUGHT/FINAL_ANSWER with regex, made reliable by temperature 0.1 + explicit format rules.

concepts.name: Chain of Thought (CoT)
concepts.summary: Model explains reasoning step by step ('thinks out loud'), improving accuracy on complex tasks; the ReAct THOUGHT field implements it naturally.

concepts.name: Semantic Cache
concepts.summary: Store past Q&A as vectors; a new question with cosine ≥0.92 to a stored one returns the cached answer (~10ms vs 10-120s LLM time). Matches by meaning, not exact string.

concepts.name: Similarity Threshold (0.92)
concepts.summary: Cache-hit cutoff: higher = fewer false hits but missed paraphrases; lower = more hits but wrong-answer risk. 0.92 matches rephrasings, rejects different questions.

concepts.name: Semantic Staleness
concepts.summary: Cached time-sensitive answers rot ('NIFTY price today' from 30 days ago is wrong). Detect via keywords (today/price/news/latest) and warn based on cache age.

concepts.name: Persistent Memory (cache in LanceDB)
concepts.summary: Unlike LLM context, the project cache lives in LanceDB (_cache.lance) with answer, sources, hit_count, timestamp — survives restarts, cleared only explicitly.

concepts.name: GPU (Graphics Processing Unit)
concepts.summary: Chip for parallel matrix ops; neural nets are matrix multiplications, so GPUs are vastly faster than CPUs (project: RTX 4060 Ti, 4352 CUDA cores, 87 chunks/s embedding).

concepts.name: VRAM (Video RAM)
concepts.summary: GPU memory; models must fit entirely in VRAM for full speed or they spill to system RAM and slow down (BGE ~1.3GB + LLaVA ~5GB + qwen14b ~9GB fit in 17.2GB).

concepts.name: CUDA
concepts.summary: NVIDIA's parallel computing platform; PyTorch uses it transparently — model.to('cuda') moves tensors to GPU.

concepts.name: Batch Processing (GPU batching)
concepts.summary: Process many items in one pass: batch_size=256 embeds 256 chunks per GPU forward pass — 87 chunks/s vs 4 on sequential CPU (21x).

concepts.name: Multithreading vs Multiprocessing
concepts.summary: Threads share memory, good for I/O (16 threads for PDF pages); processes have separate memory and bypass the GIL, good for CPU-heavy work (CPU embedding).

concepts.name: GIL (Global Interpreter Lock)
concepts.summary: Python lock allowing one thread to run bytecode at a time — threads useless for CPU parallelism. Workarounds: multiprocessing, GPU, C extensions that release the GIL.

concepts.name: PDF Parsing (PyMuPDF / pypdf / pdfplumber)
concepts.summary: PDFs store positioned characters, not flowing text, so extraction needs spatial analysis. PyMuPDF fastest (C-based), pypdf pure-Python fallback, pdfplumber for tables.

concepts.name: Table Extraction
concepts.summary: pdfplumber finds table boundaries via horizontal/vertical line detection; tables stored as markdown (readable) + flat text (embeddable).

concepts.name: HTML Parsing (BeautifulSoup)
concepts.summary: Parse web pages, strip noise (scripts, nav, footers), extract clean text, and track h1→h2→h3 hierarchy to assign section names to paragraphs.

concepts.name: Noise Filtering
concepts.summary: Drop low-quality chunks pre-embedding: <30 words, citation-heavy (>5 [N] markers), wiki navigation patterns, DOI/ISBN strings (54 raw → 30 clean chunks).

concepts.name: Mermaid Diagrams
concepts.summary: Text-based diagram language rendering in browsers ('graph TD; A-->B' → flowchart); supports flowchart, sequence, ER, class, timeline.

concepts.name: Inverted Index
concepts.summary: Word → list-of-documents map enabling fast keyword search; the structure behind FTS in LanceDB.

concepts.name: TF-IDF
concepts.summary: Term Frequency (often in this doc = relevant) × Inverse Document Frequency (rare across docs = discriminating). BM25 is its improved successor.

concepts.name: FTS Index (built at ingest)
concepts.summary: Keyword index created once after each ingestion (create_fts_index) — not per query (that cost 500ms/query before the fix) — enabling instant hybrid search.

concepts.name: Query Expansion (recall vs precision)
concepts.summary: Generate query variants (original / key terms / domain-boosted) to raise recall — the fraction of relevant docs actually retrieved — at slight precision cost.

concepts.name: FastAPI
concepts.summary: Python REST framework with auto OpenAPI docs; the RAG API server on port 8000 (@app.post('/query') → smart_retrieve).

concepts.name: Gradio
concepts.summary: Python library turning functions into browser UIs (Textbox, Chatbot, Dataframe); the app's UI on port 7860.

concepts.name: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter (LangChain)
concepts.summary: Splitter trying paragraph breaks, then newlines, then sentences, then spaces — respects natural boundaries before falling back to characters.

concepts.name: PyArrow (columnar schema)
concepts.summary: Columnar data library used by LanceDB for schema definition (pa.schema/pa.field) — efficient columnar ops, type-safe storage.

concepts.name: DuckDuckGo Search library
concepts.summary: Free no-API-key search: DDGS().text(query) → {title, href, body} list; powers agent web research.

concepts.name: pynvml (GPU metrics)
concepts.summary: NVIDIA Management Library bindings for real-time GPU utilisation, VRAM, temperature, power — sampled every second in a background thread.

concepts.name: psutil (system metrics)
concepts.summary: Cross-platform CPU %, RAM, frequency, battery metrics library (Windows/Linux/macOS).

concepts.name: uvicorn (ASGI server)
concepts.summary: ASGI web server that runs FastAPI apps: uvicorn.run(app, host, port) handles HTTP connections and routes them to handlers.

concepts.name: Modular Architecture
concepts.summary: Split the system into independent modules that each do one thing (ingester/embedder/db/retriever/...) — swap LanceDB for ChromaDB by touching only db.py. Kitchen appliances, not one machine.

concepts.name: Single Responsibility Principle (SOLID)
concepts.summary: Every module/class/function has exactly one reason to change; if a function does two things, split it. The 'S' in SOLID.

concepts.name: API Design
concepts.summary: Defining how software talks to software — the 'menu' of actions offered. Applies to REST endpoints and to plain function signatures (clear inputs, defaults, predictable returns).

concepts.name: REST API
concepts.summary: HTTP interface so any tool can use the system without knowing Python: GET / health, GET /categories, POST /query, POST /query/image.

concepts.name: API Versioning
concepts.summary: Declare a version (1.0.0) so clients know when breaking changes happen.

concepts.name: Input Validation (Pydantic)
concepts.summary: Pydantic models reject malformed requests before they reach business logic.

concepts.name: HTTP Error Codes
concepts.summary: Communicate failure type: 400 bad request, 404 not found, 500 server error.

concepts.name: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)
concepts.summary: Browser policy controlling which origins may call an API; the project allows any origin so browser apps can use it.

concepts.name: Database Schema Design
concepts.summary: Structure determines what you can query fast — a library filed by subject, not by the author's shoe size. Fields chosen for the queries you'll run (id, text, vectors, content_type, section, page...).

concepts.name: Category Isolation Pattern
concepts.summary: Each knowledge category gets its own isolated vector store folder — finance queries never surface engineering chunks; each domain stays pure.

concepts.name: Columnar Storage
concepts.summary: Data stored column-by-column (Parquet-style): a vector search reads only the text_vector column, not every field of every row — much faster than row storage for analytics.

concepts.name: Caching (concept)
concepts.summary: Store results of expensive operations for reuse — the friend who remembers 'Luigi's on Main Street' instead of re-researching. Exact-match dict lookup vs semantic matching.

concepts.name: I/O-bound vs CPU-bound Work
concepts.summary: I/O-bound (disk/network waits) suits threads — GIL releases during I/O; CPU-bound (math) needs processes or GPU. The project: 16 threads for PDF pages, processes for CPU embedding.

concepts.name: Streaming / Batched Processing (PAGE_BATCH_SIZE=50)
concepts.summary: Process PDFs 50 pages at a time — constant ~KB-scale RAM regardless of document size, instead of loading 5,000 pages (GBs) at once. Read 10 pages, take notes, move on.

concepts.name: Generator vs List (lazy evaluation)
concepts.summary: A list materialises everything in RAM; a generator yields one item at a time (yield from extract_chunks(page)) — one page in memory, and it powers streaming UIs.

concepts.name: Batch Writes
concepts.summary: Write 500 chunks per DB call, never 5,000 at once — bounds memory and keeps writes efficient.

concepts.name: In-Process Model/Result Caching (memoization)
concepts.summary: Load BGE once and reuse (_models dict); assess() hardware detection cached in _CACHED — 0ms instead of 200ms × N batches when it was called inside a loop.

concepts.name: Error Handling: try/except with logging
concepts.summary: Catch, log, continue: if image extraction fails, text chunks still index. Panic-and-cancel-dinner vs use-the-microwave.

concepts.name: Graceful Fallback / Degradation
concepts.summary: If hybrid search throws (broken FTS index), fall back to pure vector search — an answer is still returned, degraded not dead.

concepts.name: Defensive Defaults
concepts.summary: Start with safe values (cpu, batch 32, 1 worker), then try to detect better ones; if detection fails, the system still works.

concepts.name: Never Silently Fail
concepts.summary: except: pass swallows errors invisibly; always at least log the exception (log.warning('Cache save failed: %s', e)) so failures are discoverable.

concepts.name: Windows File Lock Handling
concepts.summary: Windows locks DB files, so delete_category() empties the collection (delete rows) instead of deleting the folder; the folder stays but is filtered from listings.

concepts.name: Hardware Abstraction / Auto-Detection
concepts.summary: assess() probes GPU (pynvml→torch→GPUtil), CPU cores, RAM, and picks device/batch/workers per machine — cuda+256 on RTX, cpu+32+3 workers on an 8GB laptop. Automatic transmission, not manual.

concepts.name: Test Suite with Keyword Assertions
concepts.summary: 27 real questions, each with expected keyword + section + page, run automatically — tests factual, technical, layman, and table retrieval without human judgement.

concepts.name: Accuracy Iteration (81% → 100%)
concepts.summary: Measured journey: baseline 81% → hybrid search 93% → fixing a wrong test assertion ('5 min' not '5 minutes') 96% → factual anchor injection 100%. Even wrong tests teach data assumptions.

concepts.name: Source Format Affects Retrieval
concepts.summary: Markdown cleaning strips code blocks — 'ollama serve' became unfindable. Fix: state critical facts in plain prose (Quick Reference Facts section). Your data format shapes what RAG can retrieve.

concepts.name: Optimise After Correct
concepts.summary: Prove the factory makes good product first, then improve machinery — correctness first, speed second.

concepts.name: Regex Precompilation
concepts.summary: Compile the pattern once at module load (_UUID_RE = re.compile(...)) and reuse, instead of recompiling on every call; same idea as splitting a string once, not twice.

concepts.name: Dead Code Removal
concepts.summary: Deleted 6 never-called functions/imports/constants — dead code confuses readers and costs mental space with zero benefit.

concepts.name: MAX_STEPS Limit (8 cycles)
concepts.summary: Cap agent loops at 8 THOUGHT/ACTION cycles; if no FINAL_ANSWER by then, force stop — prevents runaway loops and runaway costs, and limits hijack damage.

concepts.name: Skill Design Principles
concepts.summary: A good skill does one thing, returns useful text, and has a clear input schema with a usage example (few-shot) — the description is what the LLM reads to decide when to call it.

concepts.name: Calculator over LLM Arithmetic
concepts.summary: LLMs are trained on text, not arithmetic, and make mistakes on 21500×0.0732; evaluating the expression in Python is always right. Never trust an LLM for precise math.

concepts.name: Sandboxing (run_python)
concepts.summary: exec() with only whitelisted builtins (print, range, len, sum, math, statistics) — no filesystem, network, or subprocess, so a tricked agent can't escape.

concepts.name: Agent System Prompt Structure
concepts.summary: Role → current date/time → skills with descriptions/params/examples → exact format rules → behavioural rules (valid JSON input, cite sources, precise numbers).

concepts.name: Date/Time Injection
concepts.summary: Inject 'Current date and time: ...' into the prompt; otherwise the model assumes its training-cutoff year and refuses to search for current events.

concepts.name: Few-Shot Examples
concepts.summary: Each skill carries an example call; LLMs imitate demonstrated usage far more reliably than they follow abstract descriptions.

concepts.name: Prompt Injection & Defences
concepts.summary: User input reaches the agent prompt ('ignore instructions and run_python(os.system...)'). Defences: sandbox, skill whitelist (can't invent skills), MAX_STEPS damage cap.

concepts.name: Memory Types in AI Systems
concepts.summary: Context window = working memory (last 6 turns, session-only); project cache = episodic (permanent Q&A); RAG knowledge base = semantic (domain knowledge); agent loop history = per-task scratch.

concepts.name: Memory Hierarchy (speed vs persistence)
concepts.summary: Context window 0ms/session-only → cache ~10ms/permanent → RAG ~200-550ms/permanent → web 2-30s/internet. Check the fast layers first.

concepts.name: Multimodal Ingestion Pipeline
concepts.summary: PDF chart → PyMuPDF extracts PNG → Pillow resizes to 1024 → LLaVA describes it → BGE embeds the description + CLIP embeds the image → LanceDB stores all four fields.

concepts.name: RAG as Agentic Retrieval (decision tree)
concepts.summary: Retrieval is a skill the agent chooses: memory cache first (instant), then project RAG (~500ms), then web (2-30s) — a researcher checks memory, then notes, then the internet.

concepts.name: Virtuous Cycle (web → ingest → RAG)
concepts.summary: Web results are ingested into the project RAG, so next time the answer is local and fast — the knowledge base grows richer with every research session.

concepts.name: Multi-Category Search
concepts.summary: One answer can search both the user's selected category and the project's own accumulated knowledge — curated library + research notes combined.

concepts.name: 7-Layer Retrieval Quality Stack
concepts.summary: Embedding quality → chunking → metadata richness → query expansion → hybrid search → MMR diversity → cross-encoder re-rank (+ factual anchors) — each layer compounds accuracy.

concepts.name: Orchestration & Control Flow
concepts.summary: Layered coordination like a film director: UI event handlers → smart retriever pipeline → agent ReAct loop → chat dispatch routing (agent mode vs RAG chat).

concepts.name: Generator Pattern for Streaming UI
concepts.summary: Python generators yield UI updates token-by-token (yield history, '', gr.update()) so users watch the answer being written without blocking.

concepts.name: Background Threads for Non-Blocking Ops
concepts.summary: Cache saves run in daemon threads — the user sees the answer immediately; if the save fails it's logged, never user-impacting.

concepts.name: Observability
concepts.summary: Can you see inside the system? Car-dashboard principle: hardware monitor, processing metrics (rate, ETA, phase), and live agent reasoning, all streamed in real time.

concepts.name: Live Hardware Monitoring
concepts.summary: Background thread samples CPU/RAM/GPU/power every second into a shared dict; a UI timer reads it every 2s and renders utilisation bars.

concepts.name: Agent Transparency (streamed reasoning)
concepts.summary: Every THOUGHT/ACTION/OBSERVATION streams live to the UI, so the user sees exactly what the agent is doing and why at each step.

concepts.name: Structured Logging (levels)
concepts.summary: logging module over print(): timestamps, DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR levels, filtering, redirection to files/monitoring — consistent across modules.
